retrieved_docs.content: The cement consists of a core and matrix. The core constitutes unmixed powder particles, whereas the matrix comprises polyacid and HEMA polymer linked with hydrogen bonding.

retrieved_docs.content: Bone cement The cements currently available in the United States are polymethylmethacrylate (PMMA) and a bioactive calcium phosphate microglass cement, which is composed of 33% difunctional methacrylates and 67% bioactive glass-ceramic. The latter is much stronger than PMMA with a decreased propensity for causing exothermic reactions (60 °C versus 70 °C to 90 °C with PMMA).

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Mechanical Properties
retrieved_docs.content: The powder-to-liquid ratio determines the mechanical properties of the set cement. More powder increases the consistency of the cement, strength, and speed of the setting reaction. However, the powder-to-liquid ratio can be increased until a critical point. The volume of the physical matrix will be deficient in binding and holding the cement ingredients together; the mechanical properties will be compromised significantly. Thus, the powder-to-liquid ratio should strictly comply with the manufacturer's instructions. Using pre-graduated self-contained mixing capsules solves this problem. [14]

retrieved_docs.title: Pellucid Marginal Corneal Degeneration -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Surgical treatment comprises intracorneal ring segments (ICRS), collagen cross-linking (CXL), and partial and total corneal replacement procedures. Intracorneal ring segments are polymethyl methacrylate segments that work on the principle of addition of extra tissue in the peripheral tissue, thereby creating steeping in the periphery and flattening in the center. However, ICRS implantation is limited to specific corneal requirements like corneal thickness >450 micrometers; hence its use in PMCD is only limited to early/moderate cases. Further complications like extrusion, neovascularization along the segment, channel deposits, segment migration, epithelial ingrowth, and corneal melts limit their usage. [26]

Manipulation: It requires hydrocolloid conditioner equipment with 3 compartments to heat, store, and temper the material, and special impression trays that can circulate water. First, the material is boiled in the first compartment and changed to a sol from a gel state. In the second compartment, the material is stored at 65° Celsius and used when needed. The agar is brought to 45° Celsius in the tempering section to impress oral tissues comfortably. The water circulates through the tubing and converts the sol into a gel state. 3. retrieved_docs.content: McLean and Gasser introduced cermet in 1985. Cermet is obtained by sintering the microfine silver alloy with glass particles at high temperatures and pressure. The pellet thus formed is ground to form finer powder particles. [18]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 4 -- Community 2820
retrieved_docs.content: Cerclage (procedure) is clinically associated with قصور عنق الرحم, a condition in which the uterine cervix is unable to maintain sufficient closure during pregnancy, leading to an increased risk of preterm birth or pregnancy loss. The cerclage technique involves placing a suturing material around the cervix to reinforce its structural integrity, thereby preventing premature cervical dilation. This intervention is employed as a therapeutic measure to manage cervical insufficiency and improve obstetric outcomes for affected patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 2 -- Community 1198
retrieved_docs.content: Nasogastric tube procedures classify the nasogastric tube, indicating that the range of clinical actions, techniques, and protocols are organized around the use, insertion, management, and care of the nasogastric tube.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2421
retrieved_docs.content: Antepartum Procedures is a medical category that includes, as its child, the subcategory Antepartum Procedures: Application, Insertion, Removal, indicating that the broader group of antepartum interventions is organized into specific actions of applying, inserting, and removing devices or treatments during the prenatal period.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1895
retrieved_docs.content: Removal of cerclage from cervix is a medical intervention that is directly related to the cervical cerclage procedure (إجراء تطويق عنق الرحم). This step involves extracting the sutures that were placed around the cervix to reinforce cervical competence and prevent premature dilation, and it represents a specific phase within the overall management of cervical insufficiency. Recognizing the connection between the removal of the cerclage and the broader cerclage procedure is essential for appropriate patient care and timing of intervention.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2927
retrieved_docs.content: Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery are classified as a subset of other procedures to assist delivery, indicating that these specific interventions are encompassed within the broader category of techniques used to facilitate and support the childbirth process.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2431
retrieved_docs.content: Cervical shortening in pregnancy is categorized as a component of other complications of birth and puerperium that influence the management of the mother, and it specifically encompasses the sub‑condition of cervical shortening occurring in the second trimester.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1447
retrieved_docs.content: Cerclage (procedure) and REPRODUCTIVE HISTORY POOR are both clinically associated with قصور عنق الرحم. قصور عنق الرحم, also known as cervical insufficiency, is a condition in which the cervix is unable to maintain its structural integrity during pregnancy, increasing the risk of premature dilation and preterm delivery. The cerclage procedure involves placing a suturing stitch around the cervix to reinforce it and prevent premature opening, thereby directly addressing the mechanical weakness characteristic of قصور عنق الرحم. Additionally, a poor reproductive history—such as prior miscarriages, preterm births, or other adverse pregnancy outcomes—has been linked to a higher likelihood of developing قصور عنق الرحم, indicating that past obstetric complications can serve as a clinical indicator for this cervical disorder. Recognizing both the therapeutic role of cerclage and the predictive value of reproductive history is essential for early identification and management of patients at risk for قصور عنق الرحم.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 4 -- Community 2769
retrieved_docs.content: Low transverse cesarean section is clinically associated with تمزق الأغشية الباكر (premature rupture of membranes), indicating that this surgical approach to delivery may be linked to an increased occurrence of early membrane rupture, a factor that clinicians must consider when planning obstetric care and managing potential complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 4 -- Community 2915
retrieved_docs.content: Medical and surgical induction of labor is closely linked to the practice of artificial rupture of membranes to assist delivery, indicating that the latter serves as a primary reference point for the former. Sweeping of membrane also shares a relationship with artificial rupture of membranes to assist delivery, reflecting its role as another method employed to promote cervical ripening and facilitate labor. Together, these concepts illustrate how various mechanical and procedural interventions, including artificial rupture of membranes and membrane sweeping, are integrated within the broader framework of medical and surgical induction of labor to support successful vaginal delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 1 -- Community 141
retrieved_docs.content: of “Stabilizing induction.” “Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery” also has the specific child “فشل التوليد بالملقط” (failure of delivery with forceps). “Surgical induction of labor” is inversely related to “artificial rupture of membranes to assist delivery,” indicating that it is a specific form of that membrane‑rupturing technique. “Artificial rupture of membranes to assist delivery” is further connected to “Sweeping of membrane,” reflecting a shared relationship in membrane‑manipulation methods. Both “Medical induction of labor” and “Surgical induction of labor” are encompassed by the higher‑level category “Medical or surgical induction of labor,” which in turn has the parent category “other procedures to assist delivery.” The term “Induction by cervical dilation” is noted as related and possibly synonymous with “other procedures to assist delivery,” reinforcing its placement within this overarching group. retrieved_docs.content: positioning methods, and a spectrum of complications—including cervical dystocia, uterine atony, prolonged or stimulated labor, and pelvic‑malformation‑related difficult deliveries—providing a comprehensive framework for understanding labor‑related care and its associated indications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2409
retrieved_docs.content: Sweeping of membrane is a form of artificial rupture of membranes that is performed to assist delivery, representing a direct relationship between the technique of membrane sweeping and the obstetric practice of artificially rupturing the membranes to facilitate labor.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 1 -- Community 141
retrieved_docs.content: related and possibly synonymous with “other procedures to assist delivery,” reinforcing its placement within this overarching group. Finally, “Medical and surgical induction of labor” is a child of “Medical or surgical induction of labor” and is primarily mapped from “artificial rupture of membranes to assist delivery,” illustrating that the combined medical‑surgical approach derives from the same membrane‑rupture technique. This integrated hierarchy clarifies how various cervical procedures, induction methods, and membrane‑manipulation techniques interrelate within the context of labor induction and assisted delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 3 -- Community 1692
retrieved_docs.content: Cerclage (procedure) is clinically associated with cervical insufficiency (قصور عنق الرحم), indicating that this surgical technique is used to support a weakened cervix and prevent premature dilation in patients with this condition. In addition, optional termination of pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل - اختياري) is also clinically associated with cervical insufficiency, reflecting that pregnancy termination may be considered as a management option in severe cases where the insufficiency poses significant risk to maternal or fetal health. Both interventions are relevant clinical considerations in the assessment and treatment of cervical insufficiency.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 2 -- Community 927
retrieved_docs.content: Medical and surgical induction of labor encompasses the broader category of medical or surgical induction of labor as a child concept. The procedure of artificial rupture of membranes to assist delivery is identified as a primary mapped source for medical and surgical induction of labor, indicating that this technique is a fundamental component of labor‑induction strategies. Sweeping of membrane is also linked to artificial rupture of membranes to assist delivery, reflecting its role as a related method used to facilitate cervical ripening and promote the onset of labor. Together, these interconnected concepts illustrate how various mechanical and clinical interventions are employed in obstetric care to induce labor.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 2123
retrieved_docs.content: Induction of labor by artificial rupture of membranes is categorized as a specific child procedure within the broader group of other procedures to assist delivery, indicating that it is one of the methods used to facilitate childbirth.
